job_title: New College Grad - Semiconductor Design Engineer, DRAM Products Group
job_description:
• New College Grad - Semiconductor Verification Design Engineer, DRAM Products GroupBoise, Idaho, United States of AmericaPosted 2 months ago New College Grad - Semiconductor Design Engineer, DRAM Products GroupBoise, Idaho, United States of AmericaPosted 2 months ago Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to peers and management.
• Collaborate with Product Engineering, Test, Probe, Process Integration, Assembly, and Marketing to ensure manufacturability.
• New College Grad - Product Development Failure Analysis EngineerBoise, Idaho, United States of AmericaPosted 12 days ago New College Grad - CAD/Computer Engineer, Central EngineeringBoise, Idaho, United States of AmericaPosted 4 months ago Evaluate design architecture tradeoffs and understand their impact on power, performance, die size, and reliability.
• New College Grad - Memory Design Engineer, HBMRichardson, Texas, United States of AmericaPosted 12 days ago New College Grad - Memory Design Engineer, HBMRichardson, Texas, United States of AmericaPosted a month ago New College Grad - Memory Design Engineer, DRAMBoise, Idaho, United States of AmericaPosted 12 days ago.

• Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, Materials Science, or a related technical field Ability to manage multiple priorities, coordinate experiments, and support logistics in a dynamic environment New College Grad - Process Integration EngineerBoise, Idaho, United States of AmericaPosted 17 days ago Experience working with change management workflows and traveler or document revision processes Prior Micron experience in DRAM Process Integration, Advanced Packaging, or Change Management.
• Experience in Process Integration, semiconductor manufacturing, or module engineering support Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and inform engineering decisions Demonstrated problem‑solving skills, including SWR troubleshooting and root‑cause analysis Communicate progress, risks, and needs clearly to ensure alignment and program success Familiarity with DRAM or bond process flows and inline detection methodologies.

• Minimum QualificationsBachelor’s degree in Computer Engineering, or Computer Science.
• Programming & Tools: Python skills (Pandas, NumPy, SciPy, Scikit-learn, Matplotlib, Plotly).
• SQL and experience working with validation data warehouses.
• Visualization Skills: Must be comfortable creating custom plots and interactive exploratory visualizations using Python and other analytical tools.
• Data Science / Statistical Expertise: Ability to handle asynchronous, high-frequency hardware telemetry and create anomaly detection based algorithms.
• Ability to write clean, reproducible analytical scripts for large datasets.
